puzzle.title: The Unwavering Caller to Truth
puzzle.intro: A messenger stands alone among a stubborn people, calling them toward the One True God while they remain attached to false idols. This prophet bears a name that echoes with a powerful meaning—'the one who seeks protection'—yet receives only rejection and denial. Through gentle persuasion and firm warning, this soul carries a divine message that the universe itself will ultimately vindicate. Can you identify this steadfast figure who represents one of the most ancient narratives in Islamic scripture?
clues: My people worshipped many deities, while I called them to serve the One who created the heavens and earth.
I stood before my nation and declared that no reward would I seek from them—my only desire was their guidance to righteousness.
A great wind was sent upon my people as divine punishment, a wind of which the Quran speaks in both plural and feminine forms.
Unlike some prophets, I was not given a wife or children mentioned prominently in my narrative, for my mission was to call my people alone.
My name carries deep meaning—related to the concept of protection and refuge that one seeks.
My people were known by a specific geographical term for the valley they inhabited, and their land was described with particular disdain for its lack of vegetation.

location.label: Key Element
options: The rocky valley of the Thamud
The ancient land of the 'Ad people
location.answer: The ancient land of the 'Ad people

outcome.label: Outcome
options: My people repented and entered paradise
None of my people survived the punishment
I was taken up alive like my brother Yunus
I migrated with my followers to a blessed land
The wind destroyed only the disbelievers while believers survived
outcome.answer: None of my people survived the punishment

puzzle.arabic: كَذَّبَتْ عَادٌۭ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ عَذَابِى وَنُذُرِ
puzzle.verse: ʿAad denied; and how [severe] were My punishment and warning. (54:18)
